commands.review_summary:
The branch adds grep, find, and ls to coding tool construction, the core fs tool catalog, embedded Codex construction planning, and related agent tests.

PR surface: Source +48, Tests +57. Total +105 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main and v2026.6.10 omit grep, find, and ls from the mutable coding factory, and PR head source shows the remaining sandbox bug in the raw discovery-tool branch.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining work is a concrete branch repair: keep the new discovery tools, make sandboxed execution bridge-backed, and align public docs.

target.title: fix(agents): expose fs discovery tools to codex
target.body:
## Summary
- add ls/find/grep to the core fs tool catalog and coding tool construction
- include fs discovery tools in embedded Codex app-server construction
- update group:fs allow/deny coverage
- stabilize the slow bundle MCP tools/list timeout assertion under CI shard load

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex/OpenClaw app-server coding runs that allow `group:fs` could build `read/write/edit` but not discovery tools, so workspace listing/search fell back to shell approval instead of native `ls/find/grep` tools.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with the actual `createOpenClawCodingTools` runtime path. The same behavior was also hotpatched into Polytopic production while this upstream PR is pending.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx -e "import { createOpenClawCodingTools } from './src/agents/agent-tools.ts'; const names = createOpenClawCodingTools({ workspaceDir: process.cwd(), config: { tools: { allow: ['group:fs'], deny: ['exec', 'process', 'apply_patch'] } } }).map((tool) => tool.name); console.log(names.join('\n'));"`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - Copied console output:
    ```text
    [agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 3 tool(s) via tools.deny: apply_patch, exec, process; matched apply_patch, exec, process
    [agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 19 tool(s) via tools.allow: agents_list, create_goal, cron, gateway, get_goal, message, nodes, session_status, sessions_history, sessions_list, sessions_send, sessions_spawn, sessions_yield, skill_workshop, subagents, tts, update_goal, web_fetch, web_search
    read
    grep
    find
    ls
    edit
    write
    ```
- Observed result after fix: `group:fs` now constructs native `grep`, `find`, and `ls` tools alongside `read/edit/write` while shell/process tools remain denied. Workspace discovery no longer requires falling back to `exec` when only fs tools are approved.
- What was not tested: Full external-provider model invocation in this PR branch.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Upstream merge requires an OpenClaw maintainer; Tayoun can update the fork branch and PR but cannot merge upstream. Production Polytopic verification was done via a temporary hotpatch because upstream is still pending.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, CI/test expectations and runtime construction exposed `read/write/edit` for fs policy, while `grep/find/ls` were absent from the app-server coding tool construction path.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts src/agents/sandbox-explain.test.ts`
- `TMPDIR=/tmp node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts -t "keeps MCP tools/list responses"`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts`

Regression coverage updated for the coding tool catalog, sandbox `group:fs` expansion, app-server construction allowlists, and slow bundle MCP catalog timing.
target.author: tayoun
